TextENGNew: ``We heard the whole story from Rika-chan. ...You're all gods, right? I wish you'd give a little more thought to how your squabbles affect us humans.``
changeReason: New adds 'how your squabbles affect us humans' matching JPN あんたたちの騒ぎに巻き込まれて迷惑する人間のこと (the humans troubled by being caught up in your commotion), while old's 'the humans you're endangering' uses 'endangering' which isn't in JPN.

TextENGNew: ``It's good that we prevented it in advance. ...But this could have turned into a terrible disaster, you know?``
``It wasn't just terrible. I experienced it firsthand.``
``Anyway! It's no good for us if you gods can't get along with each other.``
``Still, they're awfully cute for gods, aren't they? ...I imagined they'd have much scarier faces.``
``Auauau! I am Oyashiro-sama! This treatment is not fair!``
``How dare you, children of man! It is outrageous to show me such disrespect and call me cute!``
``How did you see through my ambition to conquer the world?! I totally can't accept this!``
``Une-chan, why were you plotting to destroy our world in the first place?``
``Because we super love consuming everything!``
``So then what happens after you've consumed and ruined everything? How long does it take you to find another planet? And doesn't it get terribly lonely drifting through space all that time?``
``It takes long enough to drive you mad, doesn't it? And I bet you feel lonely during all that time too. ...Don't you?``
``...Nnnnnh... But... Tamura told me to get out... That super ticked me off... At least talk to me before kicking me out...``
changeReason: Old says 'At first I thought about living here peacefully' matching JPN 最初は、静かに暮らそうかと思ったのです, while new says 'At least talk to me before kicking me out' which is a substantially different sentiment not in the source text.

TextENGNew: ``Tamura's bullying is the root of all evil!``
``You all would have made the fatal mistake of letting Une escape the city!``
``So in short, Une decided to destroy the world because Tamurahime-no-Mikoto told her to leave the planet.``
``...Yes, that's right...``
``And Tamura-chan refused to talk to Hanyuu-chan, a.k.a. Oyashiro-sama, because of their falling out, and instead tried to resolve things using underhanded methods.``
``I would never stoop to asking the tainted people for help!``
``Yet the whole reason they're on bad terms in the first place is because Hanyuu-san stole some of Tamura-san's offerings, right? I believe it was just one meal?``
changeReason: New adds 'I believe it was just one meal?' which is not in the JPN for this line; JPN just says お供え泥棒に端を発する (originated from the offering theft).

TextENGNew: ``It's my fault?! I can't believe you're treating me as the main cause of all this! Auauau!!``
``Yes, yes, quiet!! We've heard the defendants' excuses! To sum up: you're all at fault because you three couldn't get along!``
``Une-chan finally reached Earth after her long, lonely journey. I think Tamura-chan could have at least tried talking to her first.``
changeReason: Old says 'could have tried to accept her a bit more. Just a bit.', new says 'could have at least tried talking to her first'; JPN 少しは受け入れてあげても means 'could have accepted her a little', closer to old.

TextENGNew: ``And Tamurahime-no-Mikoto, I understand you might resent her for eating your offering. But to stonewall Oyashiro-sama for a thousand years over it? That's going too far.``
changeReason: Old includes 'and then refuse to consult her or ask for her help in a time of crisis', which is in JPN (この肝心な時に相談も協力もせず); new says 'to stonewall Oyashiro-sama for a thousand years over it? That's going too far' which condenses and loses the crisis/consultation detail.

TextENGNew: ``For starters, this whole mess became more complicated because Hanyuu-san simply refused to apologize for stealing Tamura-san's food.``
changeReason: New adds 'for stealing Tamura-san's food' which is not explicitly in JPN for this line; JPN just says 素直に謝っていれば (if she had just apologized honestly).

TextENGNew: ``Nobody should get that furious over just one meal!!``
``I will never reconcile with that starving, empty-headed cream puff!!``
``I just wanted you to share a tiny bit of this flower bed with me. Yet you bullied me, Tamura!``
``See, see, you all heard that?! This really is Tamura's fault!``
changeReason: Old says 'See, see, did you see?!', new says 'See, see, you all heard that?!'; JPN ほらほら見ましたですか literally means 'see, see, did you see?', so old is more literal.

TextENGNew: ``Une is a devil that would annihilate everything! Une is the one to blame for all of this!!``
``...I think Tamura's hostility toward all other races started when Hanyuu stole her food. So this is actually Hanyuu's fault for starting everything.``
changeReason: Old says 'If Hanyuu hadn't done that, then Tamura wouldn't hate outsiders, and she might have accepted me', new says 'So this is actually Hanyuu's fault for starting everything'; old preserves the conditional reasoning from JPN (羽入が盗み食いをしなかったら、田村も異民族を嫌いにならず、我を受け入れてくれたかもしれない), while new condenses into a blame statement.

TextENGNew: ``Precisely! That is exactly right!! This cream-puff-for-brains is the cause of everything!!``
``I refuse to be treated as the one to blame!!!``
``Argh! Silennnnce!! Now, we shall hand down the verdict!!
All three of you are equally guilty of the crime of failing to get along, so your punishment is club game activities!!``
``Heehee. The three of you can merrily play games together and deepen your bonds, so you can avoid any future catastrophes.``
changeReason: Old includes 'Consider this a generous sentence from the priestess of Furude Shrine' matching JPN 古手神社の巫女の寛大なお沙汰よ; new omits this entirely and adds 'so you can avoid any future catastrophes' which isn't in JPN.

TextENGNew: ``Ohhohoho! You'll simply be deepening your friendship through some fun and games!``
``Once you've all enjoyed the same experiences together, I'm sure you'll get along better! I'm sure of it!``
``And it won't just be you gods! We humans will join in too, to make it a truly lively affair!``
``However! ...We club members never go easy on our opponents, do we? Kukukukuku!!``
``We'll be sure to show you plenty of our gratitude for dragging us humans into your divine squabble! Ohhohoho!``
changeReason: New adds 'Ohhohoho!' laughter which isn't in JPN; JPN doesn't have a laugh at the end of this line.

TextENGNew: ``It's no fun when you gods fight among yourselves and we can't participate! So we'll use this chance to let you feel the full brunt of our club spirit!!``
changeReason: Old includes 'So we'll take this opportunity to teach all three of you that you shouldn't take humans lightly, okay? Nihihi!' matching JPN 人間を舐めちゃいけないってことを…にっひっひ～！; new replaces with 'let you feel the full brunt of our club spirit!!' which loses the 'don't underestimate humans' message and the distinctive laugh.

TextENGNew: ``What fools! We are gods! We will not be defeated in the mere games of mankind!``
``I'll totally never lose to mere humans. Kfufufu!``
``Oh-ho? So you humans dare to challenge the gods?
Then we won't hold back either!``
Tamurahime-no-Mikoto was a steady, methodical player. Her defensive style should have made her nearly impervious to defeat.
changeReason: Old says 'steady player', new says 'steady, methodical player'; also old says 'tough opponent', new says 'nearly impervious to defeat'; JPN 手強いはず means 'should be formidable', so 'nearly impervious to defeat' overstates it.

TextENGNew: Une was likely to toy with them magnificently, using sly tricks and fluid adaptability.
changeReason: Old says 'us' (first person plural), new says 'them' (third person plural); the narrator is the one being toyed with, so 'us' matches better since the narrator is part of the group.

TextENGNew: And Hanyuu had her inscrutable dumb luck. She was the most dangerous opponent in any game involving chance.
They were gods, after all. There was no reason for them to lose to mere humans.
However... when it came to games, the club members weren't merely human either!
``Well, since Tamura-chan is the dealer, she discards first.``
``Fufufu! Behold the magnificent fortune of the gods!! I discard the green dragon and declare double riichi!``
``Ron! Hand of man, all green, four concealed triplets, single wait! That's four yakuman -- 128,000 points, straight from Tamura-chan!``
changeReason: Old says 'Pardon me!', new says 'Ron!'; in mahjong, ロン (ron) is the specific call, not 'pardon me'. Also new adds 'straight from Tamura-chan!' specifying who pays.

TextENGNew: ``Ron for me too! Hand of man, seven pairs, red, red, dora, dora -- just a simple yakuman, 32,000 points!``
``Wha?! W-W-W-W-W-W-W-W-Wha?!?!``
``Hey, Tamura. It's still too early to cry. Triple rons are allowed, right?``
``Then ron for me too! Hand of man, big four winds, all honors, four concealed triplets, single wait -- that's a six-time yakuman, 192,000 points, all from Tamura!``
``3-352,000 points straight from me?!?! T-That's impossible! Gyawawahn!!``
``Ohhohoho!! Even if we collected every point stick on the table, there wouldn't be enough to pay that! The deficit will carry over to the punishment game!``
changeReason: New adds 'The deficit will carry over to the punishment game!' which isn't explicitly in JPN for this line.

TextENGNew: ``Now!! Here's your punishment outfit---!!!``
``I-I don't understand why I must suffer such indignity!!``
``Nipah! Poor thing, poor thing.``
``You know, it's quite funny seeing you look so high and mighty in a school swimsuit.``
``Nipah! You'll be serving your master in that outfit~☆``
``Ta-da! And I'm out!``
``Ohhohoho! I'm out now too!``
``I-I'm just one card away from totally being out...!``
``Hold it! You didn't say uno!!``
``Gyawa?! Super gyawawahn!!``
``And there, I'm out too!``
``Now I'm out as well, nipah!``
``And now I'm out! Looks like last place goes to Une-chan!!``
``T-This! This outfit is super embarrassing on me!!``
``Nipah! Poor thing, poor thing.``
``It looks so good on you it's practically dangerous. Keiichi-san, aren't you going to get a nosebleed?``
``I'm sure it would be very popular with Tomitake and the others. Nipah~☆``
``You won't take me down like you did those two. Auau!!``
``Now, let the game begin! Wolf, please raise your head. Now, fortune teller, please raise your head.``
``All right! I'm revealing myself! I'm the fortune teller! Hanyuu-chan is the wolf!!``
``Auauau?!?!``
``And so, by unanimous vote, our sacrifice for the first day is...``
``Decided! We're hanging Hanyuuuuu!!``
``I-I didn't even get to play the gaaaaaaame!!``
``Kukuku! Now to have Hanyuu-chan change into her punishment outfit too!``
``Stop it! You're going to make me wear something indecent, aren't you?!``
``Something straight out of an erotic doujinshi!!``
``I promise the three of us gods will get along from now on! So spare us already!``
``I'm super repenting too!``
``I... I won't accept this!!``
